text_snippet: Chapter I Of the State, of its Territory and the Grand Duke The Grand Duchy of Luxembourg is a democratic, free, independent and indivisible State.
[Abrogated] Article 10bis 1. Luxembourgers are equal before the law. 2. They are admissible to all public, civil and military employment; the law determines the admissibility of non-Luxembourgers for
The taxes for the benefit of the State are voted annually. - The laws which establish them only have force for one year, if they are not renewed.
No privilege may be established in matters of tax. Exemption or reduction may only be established by a law.
Save in the cases formally excepted by the law, payment may be required of citizens or of public establishments, [and] only under the title of taxes for the benefit of the State or of the commune.
Any pension, affective payment [traitment d'attente] [or] any gratuity charged to the treasury may only be granted in accordance with the law.
Each year, the Chamber adopts [arrte] the law of accounts and votes the budget. All revenues and expenditures of the State must be shown in the budget and in the accounts.

1. A Court of Accounts is responsible for the control of the financial management of the organs, administrations, and services of the State; the law may confer on it the other missions of control of t
The salaries and pensions of the ministers of the religions are a charge to the State and regulated by the law. Chapter IX. Of the Communes

1. The communes form autonomous collectivities on a territorial basis, possessing juridical personality and managing by their organs their patrimony and their own interests. 2. There is in each commun

The wording of the acts of civil status and the maintaining of registers are exclusively within the attributions of the communal authorities. Chapter X. Of Public Establishments Article 108bis The law
The City of Luxembourg is the capital of the Grand Duchy and the seat of Government. The seat of the Government may only be transferred temporarily for grave reasons.

1. The State guarantees the natural rights of the human person and of the family. 2. Women and men are equal in rights and duties. The State sees to active promotion of elimination of impediments whic

1. An oath may only be imposed pursuant to the law, it determines the formula. 2. All civil public functionaries, before entering into their functions, take the following oath: "I swear fidelity to th
Every foreigner in the territory of the Grand Duchy enjoys the protection granted to persons and to property, save the exceptions established by the law.
A law, a decree or a regulation of general or communal administration is only obligatory after having been published in the form determined by the law.
No provisions of the Constitution may be suspended.

Any revision of the Constitution must be adopted in the same terms by the Chamber of Deputies in two successive votes, separated by an interval of at least three months. No revision will be adopted if

During a regency, no change can be made to the Constitution concerning the constitutional prerogatives of the Grand Duke, his status as well as the order of succession. Chapter XII. Transitory and Sup

Until provided for by a law, the Chamber of Deputies shall have the discretionary power to accuse a member of the Government, and the Superior Court of Justice, in general assembly, shall judge him, c
From the day the Constitution is executory, all the laws, all the decrees, orders, regulations and other acts which are contrary to it are abrogated.

The provisions of the Constitution are not an obstacle to the approval of the Statute of the International Criminal Court, done at Rome on 17 July 1998, and to the execution of the obligations that re
Pending the conclusion of the conventions referred to in Article 22, the current provisions concerning the religions remain in force.

Individual freedom is guaranteed. No one may be prosecuted except in the cases specified by the law and in the form which it prescribes. No one may be arrested or detained [plac] except in the cases s
Until the promulgation of the laws and regulations referred to in the Constitution the laws and regulations in force continue to be applicable.
No one may be deprived against his will of the judge that the law assigns to him.
No penalty may be established or applied except by virtue of the law.
The domicile is inviolable. An intrusion can only be made in the cases specified by the law and in the forms that it prescribes.
One may only be deprived of his property for a reason of public utility and [with] consideration [of a] just indemnity, in the case and in the manner established by the law.
The penalty of the confiscation of property may not be established.
The death penalty may not be established.

The freedom of religions, that of their public exercise, as well as the freedom to manifest religious opinions, are guaranteed, save for the repression of crimes committed on the occasion of the pract
The boundaries and chief towns of the judicial or administrative districts, of the cantons and of the communes may only be changed by virtue of a law.
No one may be forced to take part in any manner whatsoever in the acts and ceremonies of a religion or to observe its days of rest.
Civil marriage must always precede the nuptial benediction.

The intervention of the State in the nomination and the installation of the leaders of religions, the mode of nomination and dismissal of other ministers of religions, the faculty of the former or the

The State sees to the organization of primary education, which will be obligatory and free and to which access must be guaranteed to every person inhabiting the Grand Duchy. Medical and social assista

The freedom to manifest one's opinion by speech in all matters, and the freedom of the press are guaranteed, save the repression of offenses committed on the occasion of the exercise of these freedoms
The Constitution guarantees the right to assemble peacefully and unarmed in compliance with the laws that govern the exercise of this right, without having to be submitted to a prior authorization. -
The Constitution guarantees the right of association, in compliance with the laws that govern the exercise of this right without having to be submitted to a prior authorization.
Everyone has the right to address to the public authorities, petitions signed by one or more persons. - The constituted authorities alone have the right to address petitions collectively.

The secrecy of correspondence is inviolable. The law determines those agents responsible for the violation of the secrecy of correspondence entrusted to the postal [services]. The law regulates the gu
The law regulates the use of languages in administrative and judicial matters.

The Crown of the Grand Duchy is hereditary within the family of Nassau conforming to the Pact of 30 June 1783, to Article 71 of the Treaty of Vienna of 9 June 1815, and to Article 1 of the Treaty of L

No prior authorization is required to exercise proceedings against public functionaries, for faults in their administration, save that which is established in this regard [concerning] the members of t

The public functionaries, to whatever order they belong, the members of the Government excepted, may only be deprived of their functions, honors and pensions in the manner determined by the law. Chapt

1. The sovereign power resides in the Nation. The Grand Duke exercises it conforming to this Constitution and to the laws of the country. The Grand Duke exercises it conforming to this Constitution an
The Grand Duke is the Head of State, symbol of its unity and guarantor of the national independence. He exercises the executive power conforming to the Constitution and to the laws of the country.
The Grand Duke promulgates the laws within three months of the vote of the Chamber.

The Grand Duke appoints to civil and military posts in conformity to the law, and save the exceptions made by it. A salaried function can only be created by the State by virtue of a legislative provis
The Grand Duke makes the regulations and decisions necessary for the execution of the laws.
The Grand Duke makes the treaties. The treaties will not have effect before having been approved by the law and published in the forms specified for the publication of the laws. The treaties referred
The Grand Duke has the right to remit or reduce the penalties pronounced by the judges, save that which is established concerning the members of the Government.
The Grand Duke has the right to mint money in execution of the law.
The person of the grand Duke is inviolable.
The Grand Duke has the right to confer titles of nobility without ever [having] power to attach any privilege to them.
The Grand Duke confers civil and military orders[,] observing in this regard that which the law provides.

The Grand Duke may have Himself represented by a Prince of the blood, who shall bear the title of Lieutenant of the Grand Duke and who [shall] reside in the Grand Duchy. This representative shall take
The civil list is fixed at three hundred thousand gold francs per year. It may be changed by the law at the commencement of each reign. The budgetary law may allocate each year to the Sovereign House
The Grand Ducal Palace and the Chateau of Berg are reserved for the residence of the Grand Duke.
All provisions of the Grand Duke must be countersigned by a member of the Government. Section 2. Of Legislation
The assent of the Chamber of Deputies is required for each law.
The Grand Duke addresses to the Chamber the proposals or bills of law he wishes to submit to adoption. The Chamber has the right to propose bills of law to the Grand Duke.
The interpretation of the laws by way of authority may on take place by the law. Section 3. Of Justice

Justice is rendered in the name of the Grand Duke by the courts and the tribunals. The orders and judgments are executed in the name of the Grand Duke. Section 4. Of the International Powers Article 4

1. The Grand Duke of Luxembourg attains his majority on the completion of eighteen years of age. When he accedes to the throne, he takes, as soon as possible, in the presence of the Chamber of Deputie
The Chamber of Deputies represents the country. The Deputies vote without referring to their constituents and may only have in view the general interest of the Grand Duchy.

1. The Grand Duchy of Luxembourg is placed under a regime of parliamentary democracy. 2. The organization of the Chamber is regulated by law. 3. The Chamber is composed of 60 Deputies. A law adopted u

To be an elector, it is necessary: 1. to be a Luxembourger [masculine] or Luxembourger [feminine]; 2. to enjoy civil and political rights; 3. to have completed 18 years of age; To these three qualitie
[The following] may neither be electors nor eligible: 1. [persons] sentenced to criminal punishments; 2. those who, in a correctional matter, are deprived of the right to vote by [their] sentence; 3.

1. The mandate of Deputy is incompatible: with the functions of [a] member of the Government; with those of [a] member of the Council of State; with those of [a] magistrate of the Judicial Order; with
The incompatibilities specified in the preceding article are not an impediment to the others which the law establishes in the future.
The Deputies are elected for five years.

1. The Chamber verifies the credentials [pouvoirs] of its members and judge the disputes which arise on the subject. 2. On entering into their functions, they take the following oath: "I swear fidelit
The Deputy, appointed by the Government to a salaried post which he accepts, ceases immediately to sit and resumes his functions only by virtue of a new election.

All the laws are submitted to a second vote, unless the Chamber, in agreement with the Council of State, in a public sitting decides otherwise. - There shall be an interval of at least three months be
If on the death of the Grand Duke, His successor is a minor, the regency is exercised conforming to the Family Pact.
At each session, the Chamber appoints its President and its Vice Presidents and composes its Bureau.
The sittings of the Chamber are public, save the exceptions determined by its regulations.

Every resolution is taken with the absolute majority of votes. In case the votes are equally divided, the measure under deliberation is rejected. The Chamber may only take a resolution when the majori
[Abrogated]
The Chamber has the right of inquiry. The law regulates exercise of this right.
The Chamber votes on the whole of a law. The vote always takes place by roll call. At the demand of five Deputies at least, the vote on the whole of a law may proceed by a vote concerning one or more
The Chamber has the right to amend and divide the articles and amendments proposed.

It is forbidden to present petitions in person to the Chamber. The Chamber has the right to refer to the members of the Government the petitions which are addressed to it. - The members of the Governm
No action, neither civil, nor penal, can be directed against a Deputy as a result of the opinion or vote cast by him in the exercise of his functions.

With the exception of the cases foreseen by Article 68, the Deputies may be prosecuted in penal matter[s], for the duration of the session. Likewise, the arrest of a Deputy for the duration of the Ses
If the Grand Duke finds it impossible to reign, the regency is provided for as in the case of minority. In the case of vacancy of the throne, the Chamber provides for the regency provisionally. A new
The Chamber determines in it regulations the manner in which it exercises its attributions.
The sittings of the Chamber are held in the place of the residence of the administration of the Grand Duchy.

1. The Chamber meets each year in ordinary session at the time specified by [its] regulations. 2. The Grand Duke may convoke the Chamber extraordinarily; he must do so on the demand of one-third of th
The Grand Duke can dissolve the Chamber. New elections are held at the latest within three months of the dissolution.
The members of the Chamber of Deputies shall receive, in addition to their traveling expenses, an indemnity, of which the amount and conditions are determined by the law. Chapter V. Of the Government

The Grand Duke regulates the organization of his Government, which is comprised of three members at least. In the exercise of the power attributed to him by Articles 36 and 37, paragraph 4 of the Cons
The Grand Duke appoints and dismisses the members of the Government.
Members of the Government are responsible.
There is no intermediate authority between the members of the Government and the Grand Duke.

When he enters into his functions, the Regent takes the following oath: "I swear fidelity to the Grand Duke. I swear to observe the Constitution and the laws of the country." Chapter II. Public Freedo
Members of the Government have access to the Chamber and must be heard when they demand it. The Chamber may demand their presence.
In no case may an oral or written order of the Grand Duke relieve a member of the Government of his responsibility.

The Chamber has the right to accuse members of the Government. - A law shall determine the cases of responsibility, the penalties to be inflicted and the mode of procedure, either on the accusation ad
The Grand Duke may only pardon a condemned member of the Government at the request [demande] of the Chamber. Chapter Vbis. Of the Council of State Article 83bis The Council of State is called to give
Disputes which have civil rights as their object are exclusively of the resort of the tribunals.
Disputes which are political rights as their object are of the resort of the tribunals, save the exceptions established by the law.
A tribunal or a jurisdiction in contentious matters may only be established by virtue of a law. No extraordinary commissions or tribunals may be created under whatever denomination that may be.
The organization of the Superior Court of Justice is provided for by a law.
The hearings of the tribunals are public, unless such publicity is a threat to order and morality, and in which case, the tribunal so declares by a judgment.
Every judgment is substantiated. It is pronounced in public hearing.
The status of Luxembourger is acquired, retained and lost in accordance with the rules determined by civil law. This Constitution and other laws concerning political rights determine those which are,

The justices of the peace and the judges of the tribunals are directly appointed by the Grand Duke. The councillors of the Court and presidents and vice presidents of the tribunals of the districts [a

The justices of the peace, judges of the tribunals of the districts and the councillors of the Superior Court are irremovable. - Any of them may only be deprived of his post or suspended by a judgment
The salaries of members of the judicial order are specified by the law.
Save in the cases of exception specified by the law, no judge may accept salaried functions of the Government, unless he exercises them without compensation, without prejudice however to the cases of
Particular laws regulate the organization of the military tribunals, their attributions, the rights and obligations of the members of these tribunals and the duration of their functions. The law also

The courts and tribunals only apply general and local orders and regulations when these comply with the laws. - The Superior Court of Justice settles the disputes of attribution following the mode det
All which concerns the armed force is regulated by the law.
The organization and the attributions of the forces of [public] order is the subject of a law.
A Civil Guard, whose organization is regulated by the law, may be formed. Chapter VIII. Of Finances

A tax for the benefit of the State may only be established by a law. - No loan charged to the State may be contracted without the assent of the Chamber. - No real property of the State may be alienate
